questions.question: List the four fundamental components of an IoT device and state the primary function of each. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Sensor/Actuator: Interfaces with the physical world (input/output).
Microcontroller/Microprocessor: The computational brain that processes data and controls components.
Connectivity Module: Enables communication with networks/other devices.
Power Supply: Provides the necessary energy for the device to operate.

questions.question: Compare and contrast Microcontroller Units (MCUs) and Microprocessor Units (MPUs). Discuss their architecture, typical use cases in IoT, and provide one example of each. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Architecture: MCU is a SoC with integrated CPU, memory, and I/O. MPU is a standalone CPU requiring external chips.
Power Consumption: MCUs are generally more power-efficient than MPUs.
Processing Power: MPUs are typically more powerful and can run full operating systems.
Use Case: MCUs are for dedicated, low-power tasks (e.g., Arduino for a simple sensor node). MPUs are for complex processing and applications (e.g., Raspberry Pi for a smart camera).
Examples: MCU - ESP32, Arduino Uno. MPU - Raspberry Pi, BeagleBone.
